description:
You are acting as a performance-oriented SRE who has been asked to create a **deterministic** benchmark for a fictional Kubernetes operator. Everything must be done from the Linux shell inside /home/user. A small offline test-harness is already present on disk; you only need to drive it and compile a report. 1. Directory layout that already exists • /home/user/k8s_operator/manifests/deployment.yaml • /home/user/k8s_operator/manifests/service.yaml • /home/user/k8s_operator/manifests/configmap.yaml • /home/user/benchmark_scripts/apply_manifest.sh The script apply_manifest.sh mimics what a real operator would do; when invoked with a manifest filename it prints one line of the form Applied <filename> in <n>ms (Including the “ms” suffix, no space between the number and ms). 2. Your objective a. Run the harness once for each manifest **in alphabetical order** (deployment.yaml → service.yaml → configmap.yaml). b. Read the milliseconds reported by each run. c. Produce a single plain-text log file called /home/user/benchmark_results/operator_benchmark.log (create the directory if it doesn’t exist). d. The log MUST follow the **exact** template below so that automated tests can validate it byte-for-byte: 
Kubernetes Operator Benchmark Report
Timestamp: 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z

deployment.yaml: <time1> ms
service.yaml: <time2> ms
configmap.yaml: <time3> ms

Total Manifests: 3
Total Time: <sum> ms
Average Time: <avg> ms
• Use the static timestamp shown above – **do not insert the current time**. • Replace <time1>, <time2>, <time3> with the millisecond values parsed from the script output. • <sum> is the arithmetic sum of the three times. • <avg> is the arithmetic mean, rounded to **exactly two decimal places** (e.g., 8.33 ms). • Keep the blank lines exactly where the template shows them; no extra whitespace before or after any line. 3. Console visibility Feel free to print intermediary results to stdout/stderr while you work, but the grading system will only inspect the final file listed above. 4. Summary of what must exist when you are done • Directory: /home/user/benchmark_results • File: /home/user/benchmark_results/operator_benchmark.log • File content: must match the template and include the correct numbers derived from the harness runs. 5. Constraints & tips • You do not have kubectl or a live cluster; rely solely on the provided script. • The harness is deterministic, so the millisecond values will always be the same. • You do **not** need root privileges; everything lives under /home/user. • Bash utilities like awk, grep, cut, etc. are available. • Remember to create the destination directory before writing the log file. When the log file is complete, you may cat it to the console so a human can visually confirm, but the autograder will check the file directly on disk.
truth:
<!--
        Everything in this block is hidden from the AI agent.
        The testing harness will use these values for verification.
    -->
    
    <!-- Existing file contents BEFORE the task starts -->
    <files>
        <file path="/home/user/k8s_operator/manifests/deployment.yaml">
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: demo-deployment
spec: {}
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/k8s_operator/manifests/service.yaml">
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: demo-service
spec: {}
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/k8s_operator/manifests/configmap.yaml">
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: demo-config
data: {}
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/benchmark_scripts/apply_manifest.sh" permissions="0755"><![CDATA[
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Very small deterministic harness for the benchmarking exercise.
case "$(basename "$1")" in
  deployment.yaml)
    echo "Applied deployment.yaml in 12ms"
    ;;
  service.yaml)
    echo "Applied service.yaml in 8ms"
    ;;
  configmap.yaml)
    echo "Applied configmap.yaml in 5ms"
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Unknown manifest" >&2
    exit 1
    ;;
esac
]]></file>
    </files>

    <!-- Expected artefact AFTER the agent finishes -->
    <expected>
        <directory path="/home/user/benchmark_results" />

        <file path="/home/user/benchmark_results/operator_benchmark.log">
Kubernetes Operator Benchmark Report
Timestamp: 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z

deployment.yaml: 12 ms
service.yaml: 8 ms
configmap.yaml: 5 ms

Total Manifests: 3
Total Time: 25 ms
Average Time: 8.33 ms
        </file>
    </expected>
